title: Watalappan

ingredients.name: eggs
visual_checks: Use large eggs at room temperature for a smooth custard.
ingredients.notes: Use large eggs at room temperature for a smooth custard.

subs.name: Not recommended — eggs provide the custard's structure and no vegan substitute achieves the same texture.

ingredients.name: kithul palm jaggery or dark cane jaggery
visual_checks: Kithul (palm) jaggery gives an authentic deep caramel flavour; dark cane jaggery is a good substitute. Grate or roughly chop before using.
ingredients.notes: Kithul (palm) jaggery gives an authentic deep caramel flavour; dark cane jaggery is a good substitute. Grate or roughly chop before using.

subs.name: 120 g dark brown sugar or muscovado sugar dissolved in the warm coconut milk — the caramel note is present but less complex than kithul jaggery.

ingredients.name: coconut milk, full-fat
visual_checks: Warm gently to help dissolve the jaggery.
ingredients.notes: Warm gently to help dissolve the jaggery.

ingredients.name: cardamom, freshly ground
visual_checks: Freshly ground from pods gives the most fragrant result.
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground from pods gives the most fragrant result.

ingredients.name: cashews, halved
visual_checks: For garnish on top before steaming; they press into the custard and look beautiful when unmoulded.
ingredients.notes: For garnish on top before steaming; they press into the custard and look beautiful when unmoulded.

subs.name: Pistachio halves pressed into the surface create a stunning green-on-dark colour contrast for a festive presentation.

steps.text: Grate or finely chop the jaggery. Warm 200 ml of the coconut milk in a small saucepan over very low heat — do not boil. Add the jaggery and stir until completely dissolved. Remove from heat and let it cool to room temperature.

Whisk the eggs in a large bowl until the whites and yolks are fully combined but not frothy — overmixing creates bubbles that will show up as holes in the finished custard. Stir in the remaining 200 ml coconut milk.

Slowly pour the cooled jaggery-coconut milk into the egg mixture, stirring gently. Add the ground cardamom, nutmeg, and vanilla extract (if using). Pass the mixture through a fine-mesh strainer or muslin cloth into a pouring jug — this step removes any lumpy bits and gives the custard its silky finish.

Pour the strained custard into one large 20 cm round mould or six individual ramekins. If using cashews, press the halved nuts gently into the surface of the custard. Cover each mould tightly with aluminium foil so that no condensation drips onto the surface during steaming.

Bring 500 ml water to a steady simmer in a steamer or pressure cooker (with the weight off). Set the covered mould(s) on the steaming rack. Steam on low heat for 30–35 minutes for a large mould or 20–25 minutes for individual ramekins — the custard is set when the edges are firm but the centre still has a slight jiggle.

Remove from the steamer and allow to cool completely at room temperature before refrigerating for at least 1 hour. Run a thin knife around the edge of the mould and invert onto a serving plate — the cashews will be on top, pressed into the dark caramel surface. Serve chilled.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: jaggery → 120 g dark brown sugar or muscovado sugar dissolved in the warm coconut milk — the caramel note is present but less complex than kithul jaggery.; egg → Not recommended — eggs provide the custard's structure and no vegan substitute achieves the same texture.; cashew → Pistachio halves pressed into the surface create a stunning green-on-dark colour contrast for a festive presentation.
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Steam using a pressure cooker with the whistle weight removed — place ramekins on a trivet, add water, close the lid, and steam on low flame for 25 minutes. Kithul jaggery is sold at Kerala and South Indian specialty stores; Kolhapuri or Bellary dark jaggery is a widely available substitute. Coconut milk from any Indian brand works. Individual steel katoris (bowls) work as moulds in a pinch.
